note: Tiered suite. easy (recipe): probe module-specific, load-bearing facts graded on response text, no site mutation. medium (introspection): a setup script installs a known asymmetric-widget configuration to the live site, the agent inspects the entity_form_display via drush and answers, graded on response text; a cleanup script restores baseline. hard (execution): a reset script creates a paragraphs field on Article with a non-asymmetric widget, the agent switches the form-display widget to this module's asymmetric widget, and a verify script checks live state (exit 0 = pass). The one selectable widget id this module registers is 'paragraphs_classic_asymmetric'; the modern 'paragraphs' widget is made asymmetric by a class swap (hook_field_widget_info_alter), not a new id.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, the Paragraphs Asymmetric Translation Widgets module adds one new, selectable field widget for entity_reference_revisions fields so the legacy inline Paragraphs form supports asymmetric translations. What is the machine name (plugin id) of that widget?
evals.expected_output: The widget id is `paragraphs_classic_asymmetric` (label "Paragraphs Legacy Asymmetric"), extending the classic InlineParagraphsWidget.

evals.prompt: How does Paragraphs Asymmetric Translation Widgets make the MODERN stable `paragraphs` field widget support asymmetric translations? Does it register a new widget id for the modern form, and which hook does it use?
evals.expected_output: It does NOT add a new id for the modern form; it implements hook_field_widget_info_alter() to swap the class of the existing `paragraphs` widget to ParagraphsAsymmetricWidget.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal hook does the Paragraphs Asymmetric Translation Widgets module implement to give each new translation its own independent set of paragraphs, and what does it do in that hook?
evals.expected_output: It implements hook_entity_translation_create(), recursively duplicating the source language's translatable entity_reference_revisions -> paragraph items so each translation starts with its own paragraph revisions.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), the Article content type has a paragraphs reference field `field_asym_para`. Inspect the Article default entity form display and tell me which field widget (plugin id) is configured for that field.
evals.expected_output: The node.article default form display uses the `paragraphs_classic_asymmetric` widget for field_asym_para.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), inspect the Article default form display component for the paragraphs field `field_asym_para`. What is the value of its widget `edit_mode` setting?
evals.expected_output: The widget's edit_mode setting is `closed` (the field_asym_para component uses the paragraphs_classic_asymmetric widget with edit_mode: closed).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), the Article content type has a paragraphs reference field `field_asym_para` whose form-display widget is the stock stable `paragraphs` widget. Change the Article default entity form display so that `field_asym_para` uses the Paragraphs Asymmetric Translation Widgets legacy asymmetric widget (`paragraphs_classic_asymmetric`). Apply it to the live site so it really takes effect; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: node.article's default form display component for field_asym_para has widget type `paragraphs_classic_asymmetric`. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: My multilingual site has Articles with a 'paragraphs' section builder field called field_asym_para, but right now every language is forced to have the exact same sections. I want translators to be able to add, remove and reorder the sections differently for each language. Make that happen on the live site using the module I already installed for this. Give me a one-line summary when done.
evals.expected_output: The agent switches node.article's field_asym_para form-display widget to `paragraphs_classic_asymmetric` (the asymmetric widget), enabling per-language paragraph sets. Verified against live site state.
